title: Calculus I - The Definition of the Derivative
relevance: Provides the formal limit definition of the derivative and connects it to the geometric notion of a tangent line, forming the foundation for all differentiation techniques.

title: Differentiation Rules - BYJU'S
relevance: Offers clear statements and examples of the power, product, quotient, and chain rules, making it ideal for a step‑by‑step guide to basic differentiation.

title: Calculus I - Implicit Differentiation - Pauls Online Math Notes
relevance: Explains how to differentiate equations where y is not isolated, includes multiple worked examples and practice problems.

title: 3.3: Differentiation Rules - OpenStax (LibreTexts)
relevance: A textbook‑style presentation of differentiation rules with proofs and extensions to higher‑order derivatives, useful for deeper theoretical insight.

title: ALL Derivatives Rules Explained – Step‑by‑Step Examples
caption: A concise video that walks through the power, product, quotient, and chain rules with clear examples, reinforcing the written explanations.

heading: Putting It All Together: Practice and Applications
body: This sub‑section synthesizes the definitions, rules, and techniques into a cohesive workflow. Begin by expressing a function in a form amenable to the limit definition, then apply the appropriate rule(s) to compute its derivative. Use implicit differentiation when the relation cannot be solved for y explicitly. Higher‑order derivatives reveal curvature, acceleration, and inflection points. Sample problems include finding the tangent line to y = x³ − 2x at x = 1, determining dy/dx for x² + y² = 25, and computing velocity and acceleration from s(t) = 4t³ − 3t. End each exercise with a brief reflection question to solidify understanding.
